Модель использует зашумленный ViT энкодер вместе с LLM для семантического кодирования, а также отдельные диффузионные декодеры для разделения процессов декодирования. За счёт единого пространства латентных представлений модель достигает баланса между масштабируемостью генерации и семантической выразительностью понимания. Авторы также создают двойные структуры данных из одних и тех же пар изображение-текст, что помогает модели лучше использовать двойственность между задачами понимания и генерации.

en.title: UniDDT: Bridging Visual Understanding and Generation Seamlessly
en.desc: UniDDT is a novel approach to unified multimodal models that combines visual understanding and generation tasks effectively. It utilizes a Noisy ViT encoder for semantic encoding and a large language model (LLM) to enhance the integration of these tasks. By employing separate diffusion decoders, UniDDT addresses the learning conflicts and scalability issues present in existing models. The method also constructs dual data structures from image-text pairs, promoting a synergistic relationship between understanding and generation, leading to improved performance metrics across various benchmarks.

zh.title: 统一多模态模型的创新解决方案
zh.desc: UniDDT是一种新型的统一多模态模型，旨在解决视觉理解与生成任务之间的学习冲突。它利用Noisy ViT编码器和大型语言模型（LLM）进行语义编码，同时采用独立的扩散解码器来平衡视觉理解和生成任务。通过这种方法，UniDDT能够在理解和生成任务之间实现无缝兼容，提升语义一致性和可扩展性。实验结果表明，UniDDT在多模态理解和生成任务中表现出色，取得了显著的评分。

papers.title: PhoneHarness: Harnessing Phone-Use Agents through Mixed GUI, CLI, and Tool Actions
papers.abstract: Abstract PhoneHarness presents a mixed-action benchmark and execution framework for evaluating phone-use agents on verifiable mobile workflows, demonstrating superior performance over existing approaches through deterministic action routing and auditable execution traces.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Phone agents are increasingly expected to complete real mobile workflows rather than merely predict the next screen action. However, much of the current mobile-agent literature still evaluates agents primarily as GUI controllers that observe a screen, emit taps and swipes, and are scored by target app state. Real phone-use tasks are broader: they require deciding when to use app GUIs, device-side commands, or structured tools, while leaving evidence that the intended side effect actually occurred. We introduce PhoneHarness, a mixed-action benchmark and execution harness for studying phone-use agents on verifiable mobile workflows. PhoneHarness runs a device-side agent loop over GUI, CLI, and host-side tool actions, combining deterministic action routing with bounded GUI delegation and auditable execution traces. Its benchmark, PhoneHarness Bench, evaluates whether agents complete tasks with observable side effects, not only whether they produce plausible final answers. On the annotated evaluation split, PhoneHarness reaches a 75.0% pass rate, outperforming the strongest non-PhoneHarness settings by 12.9 percentage points. PhoneHarness and PhoneHarness Bench therefore play distinct but mutually dependent roles: the harness makes mixed phone workflows executable, while the benchmark measures whether agents can use that harness reliably and safely. Our findings suggest that reliable phone automation depends on action-surface routing and verifiable execution, not only visual GUI control.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong
The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen

ru.title: От визуального управления к многофункциональной мобильной автоматизации
ru.desc: PhoneHarness представляет собой бенчмарк и фреймворк для оценки агентов, которые выполняют реальные мобильные рабочие процессы на телефонах. В отличие от существующих подходов, которые оценивают агентов только по их способности взаимодействовать с GUI, PhoneHarness требует от агентов использовать различные типы действий: взаимодействие с графическим интерфейсом, командную строку и структурированные инструменты. Система использует детерминированную маршрутизацию действий и сохраняет проверяемые следы выполнения для верификации того, что задачи действительно выполнены. Бенчмарк показывает превосходные результаты (75.0% успешного выполнения), что на 12.9 процентных пункта выше альтернативных подходов.

en.title: Revolutionizing Phone Automation with PhoneHarness
en.desc: PhoneHarness is a new framework designed to evaluate phone-use agents on real mobile tasks, rather than just predicting screen actions. It allows agents to interact with various interfaces, including graphical user interfaces (GUIs) and command-line interfaces (CLIs), while ensuring that their actions can be verified. The framework includes a benchmark called PhoneHarness Bench, which assesses whether agents achieve observable outcomes from their actions. Results show that PhoneHarness significantly improves performance, indicating that effective phone automation relies on reliable action routing and verifiable execution rather than solely on visual control.

zh.title: PhoneHarness：提升手机代理的可靠性与安全性
zh.desc: PhoneHarness 是一个混合动作基准和执行框架，用于评估手机使用代理在可验证的移动工作流中的表现。与现有方法相比，它通过确定性动作路由和可审计的执行轨迹展示了更优的性能。该框架不仅关注代理的最终答案，还评估代理是否能够完成具有可观察副作用的任务。我们的研究表明，可靠的手机自动化依赖于动作表面路由和可验证的执行，而不仅仅是视觉 GUI 控制。

papers.title: Nemotron 3 Ultra: Open, Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Hybrid Mamba-Transformer Model for Agentic Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract Nemotron 3 Ultra is a large-scale language model featuring hybrid Mamba-Attention architecture with 550 billion parameters, achieving high inference throughput and extended context length through specialized training techniques.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We introduce Nemotron 3 Ultra, a 550 billion total and 55 billion active parameter Mixture-of-Experts Hybrid Mamba-Attention language model. We pre-trained Nemotron 3 Ultra on 20 trillion text tokens, then extended the context length to 1M tokens, and post-trained using Supervised Fine Tuning (SFT), Reinforcement Learning (RL), and Multi-teacher On-Policy Distillation (MOPD). Nemotron 3 Ultra is our most capable model yet, employing multiple key technologies - LatentMoE, Multi Token Prediction (MTP), NVFP4 pre-training, multi-environment RLVR, MOPD, and reasoning budget control. Nemotron 3 Ultra achieves up to ~6x higher inference throughput as compared to state-of-the-art publicly available LLMs while attaining on-par accuracy. The state-of-the-art accuracy, high inference throughput, and 1M token context length make Nemotron 3 Ultra ideal for long-running autonomous agentic tasks. We open-source the base, post-trained, and quantized checkpoints, along with the training data and recipe on HuggingFace.

ru.title: Скорость и мощь: гибридная архитектура для эффективного инфіренса
ru.desc: Nemotron 3 Ultra — это большая языковая модель с 550 миллиардами параметров, использующая гибридную архитектуру Mamba-Attention и механизм Mixture-of-Experts для эффективной обработки информации. Модель была предварительно обучена на 20 триллионах текстовых токенов и расширена до контекстного окна в 1 миллион токенов благодаря специализированным методам обучения. Для улучшения качества использовались Supervised Fine Tuning, Reinforcement Learning и Multi-teacher On-Policy Distillation, что позволило достичь лучших результатов в точности. Особенность модели заключается в том, что она обеспечивает примерно в 6 раз более высокую пропускную способность при инференции по сравнению с существующими LLM, что делает её идеальной для долгоживущих автономных задач.

en.title: Unlocking Language with Unmatched Scale and Speed
en.desc: Nemotron 3 Ultra is a powerful language model that uses a unique Mamba-Attention architecture with a total of 550 billion parameters. It has been trained on a massive dataset of 20 trillion text tokens, allowing it to handle very long contexts of up to 1 million tokens. The model employs advanced techniques like Supervised Fine Tuning and Reinforcement Learning to enhance its performance. With significantly improved inference speed and accuracy, Nemotron 3 Ultra is designed for complex tasks requiring long-term reasoning and decision-making.

zh.title: Nemotron 3 Ultra：高效能的语言模型新纪元
zh.desc: Nemotron 3 Ultra 是一种大型语言模型，采用混合 Mamba-Attention 架构，拥有 5500 亿个参数。它通过专门的训练技术实现了高推理吞吐量和扩展的上下文长度，能够处理多达 100 万个标记的文本。该模型经过 20 万亿文本标记的预训练，并使用监督微调、强化学习和多教师在线蒸馏等方法进行后训练。Nemotron 3 Ultra 在推理速度和准确性方面均表现出色，非常适合长时间运行的自主任务。

papers.title: Hierarchical Advantage Weighting for Online RL Fine-Tuning of VLAs from Sparse Episode Outcomes
papers.abstract: Abstract Hierarchical Advantage-Weighted Behavior Cloning (HABC) addresses sparse reward challenges in robot learning by separately optimizing viability and efficiency objectives through adaptive critic heads and intervention-aware credit assignment, significantly improving success rates in contact-rich manipulation tasks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct When pretrained VLA policies are fine-tuned through online RL, each rollout episode produces only a single binary outcome (success or failure), yet the actor update requires per-transition supervision. Existing approaches commonly reduce this sparse outcome to a single scalar reward or advantage signal, which conflates distinct forms of transition-level feedback and provides limited guidance once basic task success becomes achievable. First, a single scalar signal conflates the two objectives of viability and efficiency; once basic success is achieved, the binary label provides no gradient to distinguish efficient completions from slow ones. Second, real-world rollouts mix autonomous and intervention segments; naively assigning episode outcomes across these boundaries introduces incorrect credit assignment. To address these issues, we propose Hierarchical Advantage-Weighted Behavior Cloning (HABC), which trains separate critic heads for these two objectives on different data subsets and combines their outputs with a state-adaptive balance. A state-adaptive gate g_t merges their one-step advantages, prioritizing viability when success is uncertain and shifting to efficiency only when viability is high, and converts the result into per-transition weights on the actor loss. Intervention-aware credit assignment further restricts outcome labels to segments executed by the current policy, preventing supervision from leaking across intervention boundaries. In real-robot experiments on three contact-rich bimanual tasks, HABC raises success from supervised fine-tuning (SFT) baselines of 36%, 44%, and 12% to 92%, 88%, and 38%.
affiliations: Shenzhen International Graduate School, Tsinghua University
The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Раздельная оптимизация достижимости и эффективности в обучении робота
ru.desc: Статья представляет метод Hierarchical Advantage-Weighted Behavior Cloning (HABC) для решения проблемы разреженных вознаграждений при обучении робототехнических систем. Ключевая идея заключается в раздельной оптимизации двух целей — достижимости (viability) и эффективности (efficiency) — с использованием адаптивных критических голов и взвешивания преимуществ на уровне переходов. Метод включает механизм адаптивного баланса состояния, который приоритизирует достижимость при неуверенности в успехе и переходит к оптимизации эффективности после достижения базового успеха. Интервенция-осведомленное распределение кредита предотвращает утечку обучающего сигнала через границы вмешательства человека, что особенно важно для контактного манипулирования в реальных условиях.

en.title: Boosting Robot Learning with HABC: Viability Meets Efficiency!
en.desc: Hierarchical Advantage-Weighted Behavior Cloning (HABC) is a method designed to improve robot learning in environments with sparse rewards. It does this by optimizing two key objectives: viability, which ensures the robot can complete tasks, and efficiency, which focuses on how quickly tasks are completed. HABC uses separate critic heads to evaluate these objectives and employs a state-adaptive mechanism to balance their contributions based on the current task context. This approach significantly enhances success rates in complex manipulation tasks by providing clearer guidance during training and preventing errors in credit assignment during mixed-autonomous and intervention phases.

zh.title: 提升机器人学习成功率的层次方法
zh.desc: 层次优势加权行为克隆（HABC）通过自适应评论头和干预感知的信用分配，分别优化可行性和效率目标，从而解决机器人学习中的稀疏奖励挑战。这种方法显著提高了在接触丰富的操作任务中的成功率。HABC通过对不同数据子集训练独立的评论头，并结合它们的输出，来实现对这两个目标的优化。实验结果表明，HABC在三项任务中的成功率显著高于传统的监督微调基线。

papers.title: MVEB: Massive Video Embedding Benchmark
papers.abstract: Abstract A large-scale video embedding benchmark evaluates diverse models across multiple video understanding tasks, revealing that different model architectures excel in specific domains and demonstrating the nuanced impact of audio on performance based on dataset characteristics.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We introduce the Massive Video Embedding Benchmark (MVEB), a 23-task benchmark for video embeddings spanning classification, zero-shot classification, clustering, pair classification, retrieval, and video-centric question answering. We evaluate 33 models and find that no single model dominates: MLLM-based embeddings lead on classification, clustering, pair classification, and QA; multimodal binding leads on retrieval and zero-shot classification; generative MLLMs without contrastive adaptation collapse on cross-modal tasks. Paired video-only vs. audio+video evaluations show that audio's contribution depends on dataset annotation provenance: audio helps when labels were produced from both modalities and hurts when they were produced from visuals alone, a six-point gap consistent across model families. MVEB is derived from MVEB+, a 184-task pool, and is designed to maintain task diversity while reducing evaluation cost. It integrates into the MTEB ecosystem for unified evaluation across text, image, audio, and video. We release MVEB and all 184 tasks along with code and a leaderboard at https://github.com/embeddings-benchmark/mteb.
affiliations: Shanghai University of Finance and Economics

ru.title: Нет универсальной модели для видео-эмбеддингов: специализация архитектур по задачам
ru.desc: Авторы представляют большой бенчмарк MVEB для оценки видео-эмбеддингов на 23 различных задачах, включая классификацию, кластеризацию, поиск и вопросно-ответные системы. Они протестировали 33 модели и обнаружили, что разные архитектуры показывают лучшие результаты на разных типах задач: модели на основе больших мультимодальных языковых моделей лидируют в классификации, а мультимодальное связывание показывает лучший результат в retrieval. Интересный вывод заключается в том, что влияние аудио на производительность зависит от того, как были созданы разметки датасета: аудио помогает, когда метки создавались с использованием обеих модальностей, и наоборот, если метки были получены только из видео. Бенчмарк создан на основе 184 задач и интегрирован в экосистему MTEB для единой оценки эмбеддингов разных модальностей.

en.title: Unlocking Video Understanding: The Power of Diverse Models and Audio Insights
en.desc: The Massive Video Embedding Benchmark (MVEB) is a comprehensive evaluation framework for video understanding tasks, consisting of 23 distinct tasks such as classification and retrieval. It assesses 33 different models, revealing that no single model excels across all tasks; instead, specific architectures perform better in certain areas. The study highlights the importance of audio in video tasks, showing that its effectiveness varies based on how the dataset labels were created. MVEB aims to provide a diverse yet cost-effective evaluation method, contributing to the broader MTEB ecosystem for multimodal assessments.

zh.title: 视频理解的多样性与音频影响
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一个名为大规模视频嵌入基准（MVEB）的新基准，涵盖了23个视频理解任务，包括分类、零样本分类、聚类等。研究发现，不同的模型在特定任务上表现优异，且音频对性能的影响与数据集特征密切相关。通过对33个模型的评估，发现没有单一模型在所有任务中占据主导地位，且音频的贡献取决于标签的生成方式。MVEB旨在保持任务多样性，同时降低评估成本，并与MTEB生态系统整合，实现跨文本、图像、音频和视频的统一评估。

papers.title: Unstable Features, Reproducible Subspaces: Understanding Seed Dependence in Sparse Autoencoders
papers.abstract: Abstract Sparse autoencoders exhibit feature stability patterns where stable features carry most predictive signal while unstable features reflect reproducible low-dimensional structure despite individual non-reproducibility.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Sparse autoencoders (SAEs) are widely used to interpret neural network representations, but their utility depends on whether the learned features are reproducible across training runs. We study this question through feature stability: for each SAE feature, we estimate the probability that a similar feature reappears in an independently trained SAE. This yields a scalable per-feature signal that separates stable from unstable features. In a large-scale study across seeds, models, layers, dictionary sizes, and SAE variants, we find a pronounced functional asymmetry: stable features carry most of the reconstruction- and prediction-relevant signal, while unstable features have weak marginal impact and are dominated by low-frequency surface-form triggers in both activation statistics and automatic explanations. Geometrically, unstable features are individually non-reproducible but concentrate in reproducible lower-rank subspaces, suggesting that seed dependence often reflects basis ambiguity within a shared region of activation space rather than pure noise. A controlled synthetic model makes this mechanism explicit, showing that low-rank ground-truth features can be recovered at the subspace level while remaining non-identifiable as individual SAE latents across seeds. Finally, by pooling unique cross-seed features, we construct more stable SAEs while preserving explained variance in this setting. Together, these results show that unstable features are not merely failed or noisy latents: they have weak individual functional impact, but reflect reproducible low-dimensional structure that standard SAEs resolve differently across seeds.

ru.title: Стабильность признаков в разреженных автокодировщиках: от шума к структуре
ru.desc: В работе исследуется стабильность признаков в разреженных автокодировщиках (SAE) — нейросетевых моделях для интерпретации представлений. Авторы показывают, что стабильные признаки, которые воспроизводятся при переобучении модели, несут основной сигнал для реконструкции и предсказания, тогда как нестабильные признаки имеют слабое индивидуальное влияние. Несмотря на то что нестабильные признаки не воспроизводятся как отдельные латентные переменные, они сосредоточены в воспроизводимых низкоранговых подпространствах, отражая структурированные закономерности в пространстве активаций. Исследование предлагает методы построения более стабильных автокодировщиков путём объединения уникальных признаков из разных реализаций обучения.

en.title: Stability in Sparse Autoencoders: Harnessing Predictive Power from Reproducible Features
en.desc: This paper investigates the stability of features learned by sparse autoencoders (SAEs) across different training runs. It finds that stable features are crucial for making accurate predictions, while unstable features, although reproducible in a low-dimensional space, contribute less to the overall predictive power. The study reveals that unstable features can be seen as reflections of shared activation patterns rather than random noise. By combining unique features from different training seeds, the authors propose a method to enhance the stability of SAEs without losing important information.

zh.title: 稳定特征与不稳定特征的深度解析
zh.desc: 稀疏自编码器（SAE）在特征稳定性方面表现出独特的模式，稳定特征承载了大部分的预测信号，而不稳定特征则反映了可重复的低维结构。我们通过特征稳定性研究SAE特征的可重复性，估计每个特征在独立训练的SAE中重新出现的概率。研究发现，稳定特征在重建和预测中起着重要作用，而不稳定特征的影响较弱，主要受低频表面触发因素的影响。最终，通过汇聚独特的跨种子特征，我们构建了更稳定的SAE，同时保持了解释方差。

papers.title: MMDiff: Extending Diffusion Transformers for Multi-Modal Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract MMDiff transforms frozen diffusion transformers into multi-modal generative systems that produce images and perceptual modalities using lightweight decoders, achieving improved semantic segmentation through multi-timestep feature fusion and spatial aggregation.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Diffusion transformers have demonstrated remarkable generative capabilities, yet the rich perceptual representations computed across their denoising trajectory are discarded once the content is rendered. We present MMDiff, a framework that transforms a frozen diffusion transformer into a multi-modal generative system that jointly produces images alongside any combination of dense perceptual modalities using lightweight decoder heads. Our central finding is that perceptual information is temporally distributed along the denoising trajectory, and that multi-timestep feature fusion with spatially varying aggregation weights is essential, improving semantic segmentation results by up to 28.7% mIoU over single-timestep extraction. We further adopt concept-driven attention extraction for interpretable spatial guidance, and show that frozen diffusion features are competitive with and complementary to state-of-the-art encoders such as DINOv3. By training only lightweight decoder heads on a frozen backbone, we achieve strong performance in semantic segmentation, salient object detection, and depth estimation, and demonstrate that this framework enables effective synthetic data generation at scale.
affiliations: University of Oxford, Visual Geometry Group

ru.title: Многомодальная генерация из замороженного диффузионного трансформера
ru.desc: MMDiff — это фреймворк, который превращает замороженные диффузионные трансформеры в мультимодальные генеративные системы, способные одновременно производить изображения и перцептивные модальности через лёгкие декодеры. Ключевое открытие заключается в том, что перцептивная информация распределена временно вдоль траектории денойзинга, и многошаговое слияние признаков со пространственно-зависимой агрегацией критически улучшает результаты семантической сегментации на 28.7% mIoU. Авторы применяют концепт-ориентированную экстракцию внимания для интерпретируемого пространственного руководства и демонстрируют, что замороженные признаки диффузии конкурентоспособны с продвинутыми энкодерами типа DINOv3. Путём обучения только лёгких декодеров на замороженном основании достигаются сильные результаты в семантической сегментации, детекции выделяющихся объектов и оценке глубины.

en.title: Transforming Diffusion Transformers for Multi-Modal Generation
en.desc: MMDiff is a framework that enhances frozen diffusion transformers, allowing them to generate images and various perceptual modalities simultaneously. It utilizes lightweight decoders to retain and utilize rich perceptual information throughout the denoising process, rather than discarding it after rendering. By employing multi-timestep feature fusion and spatial aggregation, MMDiff significantly improves semantic segmentation performance, achieving up to 28.7% better results compared to traditional methods. This approach also incorporates concept-driven attention for better interpretability and shows that the features from frozen diffusion models can compete with advanced encoders like DINOv3.

zh.title: MMDiff：多模态生成的创新框架
zh.desc: MMDiff是一种将冻结的扩散变换器转变为多模态生成系统的框架。它使用轻量级解码器生成图像和感知模态，并通过多时间步特征融合和空间聚合来提高语义分割的效果。研究发现，感知信息在去噪轨迹中是时间分布的，采用多时间步特征融合可以显著提升语义分割的准确性。通过仅训练轻量级解码器，MMDiff在语义分割、显著性物体检测和深度估计等任务中表现出色，并能够有效生成大规模合成数据。

papers.title: You Don't Need Strong Assumptions: Visual Representation Learning via Temporal Differences
papers.abstract: Abstract Temporal Difference in Vision (TDV) presents a novel self-supervised learning approach for video data that eliminates traditional inductive biases by leveraging causal relationships between past and future frames.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Progress in AI has largely been driven by methods that assume less. As compute and data increase, approaches with weaker inductive biases generally outperform those with stronger assumptions. This is particularly characteristic of the field of Visual Representation Learning, where approaches have gone from being dominated by Supervised Learning, to Weakly Supervised Learning, to the now widespread success of Self-Supervised Learning without human labels. Yet, even modern Self-Supervised Learning approaches still depend on strong inductive biases such as augmentations, masking, or cropping. If this trend holds, even these remaining biases should become bottlenecks at scale -- and our experiments confirm this: the optimal strength of inductive biases decreases as data grows. This motivates the search for approaches that rely on fewer assumptions. To this end, we introduce Temporal Difference in Vision (TDV), a new paradigm for self-supervised learning from video that avoids existing inductive biases, relying instead on a causal assumption that the past causes the future. TDV functions by jointly training an image encoder and a motion encoder so that the current frame's representation plus the encoded motion equals the next frame's representation. Despite not leveraging any strong inductive biases, TDV matches state-of-the-art recipes on dense spatial tasks, laying the foundation for representation learning without strong assumptions.

ru.title: Обучение видеопредставлений без индуктивных смещений через причинные отношения
ru.desc: В работе представляется новый подход к самообучению на видеоданных под названием Temporal Difference in Vision (TDV), который отказывается от традиционных индуктивных смещений и опирается на причинную связь между прошлыми и будущими кадрами. Авторы показывают, что с увеличением объёма данных и вычислительных ресурсов методы с менее сильными предположениями работают лучше, поэтому даже современные подходы к самообучению с аугментациями и маскированием могут стать узкими местами. TDV обучает энкодер изображений и энкодер движения совместно так, чтобы представление текущего кадра плюс закодированное движение равнялись представлению следующего кадра. Несмотря на отсутствие явных индуктивных смещений, TDV достигает результатов, соответствующих лучшим существующим методам на задачах плотного анализа пространственных данных.

en.title: Learning Video Representation Without Strong Assumptions
en.desc: Temporal Difference in Vision (TDV) introduces a self-supervised learning method for video data that minimizes reliance on traditional inductive biases. It leverages the causal relationship between past and future frames, allowing the model to learn without strong assumptions. By jointly training an image encoder and a motion encoder, TDV ensures that the representation of the current frame combined with motion information predicts the next frame's representation. This approach achieves competitive performance on dense spatial tasks, suggesting a promising direction for representation learning that reduces dependency on strong biases as data scales up.

zh.title: 无偏见的视频自监督学习新方法
zh.desc: 时间差异视觉（TDV）是一种新颖的自监督学习方法，专注于视频数据，旨在消除传统的归纳偏见。该方法利用过去和未来帧之间的因果关系，通过联合训练图像编码器和运动编码器来实现。TDV的核心思想是当前帧的表示加上编码的运动等于下一帧的表示。尽管不依赖于强归纳偏见，TDV在密集空间任务上仍能与最先进的方法相媲美，为无强假设的表示学习奠定了基础。

papers.title: Track2View: 4D-Consistent Camera-Controlled Video Generation via Paired 3D Point Tracks
papers.abstract: Abstract Track2View generates novel camera viewpoints from videos by using 3D point tracks to establish explicit spatiotemporal correspondences, achieving superior visual quality and camera accuracy compared to existing methods.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Re-rendering an existing video from a novel camera viewpoint requires the output to follow the prescribed camera trajectory while preserving the appearance and dynamics of the original scene across every frame. Existing methods rely on per-frame pose embeddings, noisy point-cloud renderings, or implicit learned correspondences, none of which provides an explicit, temporally continuous link between source and target pixels. We propose Track2View, which conditions a video diffusion transformer on paired 3D point tracks: sparse trajectories of scene points projected into both the source and target camera views. These tracks provide explicit spatiotemporal correspondences that are temporally continuous by construction, encoding what content should appear where and when. At the core of Track2View is a dual-view track conditioner that transfers visual context from source to target view through parameter-free geometric operations and learned temporal aggregation, ensuring generalization to arbitrary camera trajectories without memorizing specific motions. We further introduce a data curation pipeline that extracts one-to-one track correspondences by running a 3D point tracker on temporally concatenated multi-camera view pairs. On a 400-video benchmark spanning static and dynamic scenes, Track2View achieves state-of-the-art results across visual quality, view synchronization, and camera accuracy, reducing rotation error by 30-65% and translation error by 61-72% relative to leading baselines. Project page is available at this https URL: https://qjizhi.github.io/track2view

ru.title: Явные пространственно-временные соответствия для синтеза видео с произвольной камерой
ru.desc: Track2View — это метод для генерации новых ракурсов видео с помощью 3D трекинга точек сцены, которые обеспечивают явные пространственно-временные соответствия между исходным и целевым кадрами. Авторы используют диффузионный трансформер для видео, кондиционированный на парах 3D треков, которые передают визуальный контекст через геометрические операции и обучаемую временную агрегацию. Метод генерализуется на произвольные траектории камеры без запоминания специфических движений благодаря использованию явных временно-непрерывных соответствий. На бенчмарке из 400 видеороликов Track2View достигает состояния искусства по качеству синтеза, синхронизации представлений и точности камеры, снижая ошибки ротации на 30-65% и трансляции на 61-72% по сравнению с конкурирующими методами.

en.title: Transforming Video Perspectives with Precision
en.desc: Track2View is a machine learning method that generates new camera viewpoints from existing videos by using 3D point tracks to create clear connections between the source and target views. Unlike previous techniques that struggle with maintaining continuity, Track2View uses paired 3D point tracks to ensure that the visual content aligns correctly over time. This approach allows for better visual quality and accuracy in camera movements, as it leverages geometric operations and learned temporal aggregation without needing to memorize specific motions. In tests, Track2View outperformed other methods, significantly reducing errors in camera rotation and translation while maintaining high visual fidelity.

zh.title: Track2View：新视角视频生成的创新方法
zh.desc: Track2View 是一种通过使用 3D 点轨迹生成新视角视频的方法。它通过建立明确的时空对应关系，显著提高了视觉质量和相机精度。与现有方法不同，Track2View 利用配对的 3D 点轨迹来确保源视图和目标视图之间的连续性。该方法在多个视频基准测试中表现出色，减少了旋转和位移误差。

papers.title: Prompt-Level Distillation: A Non-Parametric Alternative to Model Fine-Tuning for Efficient Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract Prompt-Level Distillation extracts reasoning patterns from teacher models to enhance student model performance while maintaining interpretability and reducing latency.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Advanced reasoning typically requires Chain-of-Thought prompting, which is accurate but incurs prohibitive latency and substantial test-time inference costs. The standard alternative, fine-tuning smaller models, often sacrifices interpretability while introducing significant resource and operational overhead. To address these limitations, we introduce Prompt-Level Distillation (PLD). We extract explicit reasoning patterns from a Teacher model and organize them into a structured list of expressive instructions for the Student model's System Prompt. Evaluated using Gemma-3 4B, PLD improved Macro F1 scores on StereoSet (57\% to 90.0\%) and Contract-NLI (67\% to 83\%), while increasing LogiQA accuracy to 70\%. Similar results on Mistral Small 3.1 demonstrate cross-architecture generalizability, enabling these compact models to match frontier performance with negligible latency overhead. These expressive instructions render the decision-making process transparent, allowing for full human verification of logic, making this approach ideal for regulated industries such as law, finance, and content moderation, as well as high-volume use cases and edge devices.

ru.title: Передача знаний через явные инструкции: интерпретируемая дистилляция для компактных моделей
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод Prompt-Level Distillation, который извлекает паттерны рассуждений из больших учительских моделей и передает их компактным студентским моделям через структурированные инструкции в system prompt. Метод позволяет значительно улучшить качество работы малых моделей на задачах классификации и логического вывода, одновременно снижая латентность и вычислительные затраты. Ключевое преимущество подхода заключается в сохранении интерпретируемости решений, позволяя человеку полностью проверить логику работы модели. Метод демонстрирует обобщаемость на разные архитектуры и особенно полезен для регулируемых отраслей и развертывания на периферийных устройствах.

en.title: Enhancing Student Models with Efficient Reasoning Patterns
en.desc: This paper presents Prompt-Level Distillation (PLD), a method that improves the performance of student models by extracting reasoning patterns from teacher models. PLD organizes these patterns into structured instructions that enhance the interpretability and efficiency of the student model, reducing latency during inference. The approach has shown significant improvements in various benchmarks, such as increasing Macro F1 scores and LogiQA accuracy, while maintaining low resource usage. This makes PLD particularly suitable for applications in regulated industries where transparency and efficiency are crucial.

zh.title: 提升模型性能的提示级蒸馏方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为提示级蒸馏（Prompt-Level Distillation, PLD）的方法，旨在从教师模型中提取推理模式，以提高学生模型的性能，同时保持可解释性并减少延迟。通过将推理模式组织成结构化的指令列表，PLD能够有效地指导学生模型的系统提示。实验结果表明，PLD在多个基准测试中显著提高了模型的准确性，并且在不同架构之间具有良好的通用性。该方法特别适用于法律、金融和内容审核等受监管行业，以及高频使用场景和边缘设备。

papers.title: Artificial Intelligence Index Report 2026
papers.abstract: Abstract  Welcome to the ninth edition of the AI Index report. As AI continues to advance rapidly, the question becomes whether the systems built around it can keep up. Governance frameworks, evaluation methods, education systems, and the data infrastructure needed to track AI's impact are struggling to match the pace of the technology itself. That gap between what AI can do and how prepared we are to manage it runs through every chapter of this year's report. New in this edition, the report tracks how AI is being tested more ambitiously across reasoning, safety, and real-world task execution, and why those measurements are increasingly difficult to rely on. It also features new estimates of generative AI's economic value alongside emerging evidence of its labor market effects, an analytical framework on AI sovereignty, and a science chapter developed in collaboration with Schmidt Sciences. For the first time, the report features standalone chapters on AI in science and AI in medicine, reflecting AI's growing impact across these two domains.
affiliations: Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence (HAI)

ru.title: Разрыв между возможностями AI и готовностью общества управлять ними
ru.desc: Доклад AI Index посвящен анализу разрыва между быстрым развитием систем искусственного интеллекта и неготовностью общества управлять этими технологиями. В отчете рассматриваются новые методы оценки LLM и нейросетей по критериям рассуждения, безопасности и выполнения реальных задач, а также отмечается снижение надежности таких метрик. Авторы представляют оценки экономической стоимости генеративного AI, анализируют влияние на рынок труда и разработали аналитическую базу для понимания AI суверенитета. Впервые в истории отчета выделены отдельные главы о применении AI в научных исследованиях и медицине, демонстрируя растущее значение этих технологий в критических областях.

en.title: Bridging the Gap: Managing AI's Rapid Growth
en.desc: The AI Index report highlights the rapid advancements in artificial intelligence and the challenges in governance and evaluation that accompany this growth. It emphasizes the widening gap between AI capabilities and our preparedness to manage its implications effectively. The report introduces new metrics for assessing AI's performance in reasoning, safety, and real-world applications, while also addressing the difficulties in relying on these measurements. Additionally, it explores the economic impact of generative AI and its effects on the labor market, along with dedicated sections on AI's influence in science and medicine.

zh.title: 人工智能的快速发展与管理挑战
zh.desc: 本报告是AI指数的第九版，探讨了人工智能的快速发展与管理框架之间的差距。报告指出，治理框架、评估方法和教育系统未能跟上技术的进步，导致我们在管理AI影响时面临挑战。新版本中，报告更深入地跟踪了AI在推理、安全性和实际任务执行方面的测试情况，并分析了这些测量的可靠性问题。此外，报告首次独立讨论了AI在科学和医学领域的应用，反映了AI在这些领域日益增长的影响力。

papers.title: PermaVid: Consistent Video Generation Across Edits via Disentangled Context Memory
papers.abstract: Abstract PermaVid addresses long-term video consistency after edits by using multi-modal memory banks that separate appearance and geometric structure, enabling coherent video generation across time and viewpoints.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Consistent video generation under editing operations requires persistence: when edits modify scene appearance or layout, subsequent generations should remain coherent across time and viewpoints. However, existing memory designs struggle to maintain long-term consistency after such modifications, as stored contexts may become outdated or invalid. To address this, we propose PermaVid, a novel framework built upon a multi-modal context memory that disentangles spatial context into semantic appearance and geometric structure, together with an edit-aware memory update and retrieval strategy that keeps memory evolution aligned with subsequent observations. Specifically, we develop two complementary memory banks: an RGB context memory that captures appearance-aware observations while implicitly encoding geometry, and a depth context memory that preserves geometry-only structure disentangled from semantics. Building on this design, we introduce a memory-guided video generation model that performs multi-modal feature fusion under reference conditions drawn from mixed-modality memory contexts. Experiments demonstrate that our method maintains strong long-term semantic and structural consistency after edits, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art methods.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Мультимодальная память для стабильной генерации видео после редактирования
ru.desc: PermaVid — это фреймворк для генерации видео, который сохраняет согласованность сцены после редактирования благодаря использованию мультимодальных банков памяти. Система разделяет контекст на две части: банк RGB-памяти для захвата семантического описания внешнего вида и банк глубины для сохранения геометрической структуры сцены. Фреймворк использует стратегию обновления и извлечения памяти, которая адаптируется к внесённым изменениям и остаётся согласованной с последующими наблюдениями. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что метод значительно превосходит существующие подходы в поддержании долгосрочной семантической и структурной согласованности видео.

en.title: Ensuring Long-Term Video Consistency with PermaVid
en.desc: PermaVid is a framework designed to ensure that videos remain consistent over time, even after edits. It uses multi-modal memory banks that separate visual appearance from geometric structure, allowing for coherent video generation across different viewpoints. The framework includes a unique memory update strategy that adapts to changes in the scene, ensuring that the stored information remains relevant. Experiments show that PermaVid outperforms existing methods in maintaining both semantic and structural consistency in edited videos.

zh.title: PermaVid：实现视频编辑后的长期一致性
zh.desc: PermaVid 是一个新颖的框架，旨在解决视频编辑后的长期一致性问题。它通过多模态记忆库将外观和几何结构分开，从而实现跨时间和视角的一致视频生成。该方法引入了编辑感知的记忆更新和检索策略，确保记忆的演变与后续观察保持一致。实验结果表明，PermaVid 在编辑后能够保持强大的语义和结构一致性，显著优于现有的最先进方法。

papers.title: ExpRL: Exploratory RL for LLM Mid-Training
papers.abstract: Abstract ExpRL uses human-written question-answer data as reward scaffolds to provide automated reinforcement learning priming for language models, outperforming traditional methods on math reasoning tasks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Sparse reward reinforcement learning (RL) has become a standard tool for improving LLM reasoning, but its success depends critically on the coverage present in the base model. In practice, models are often primed for RL through mid-training on curated reasoning traces that teach useful primitive skills such as decomposition, verification, or self-correction. Although effective, this strategy requires manually specifying what the model should learn, and it remains unclear whether such primitive coverage is enough for much harder problems, which require combining these skills into broader solution strategies. We study a more automated approach: RL-based mid-training using large corpora of human-written question-answer data. Rather than treating reference solutions as targets to imitate, our method, ExpRL, uses them as reward scaffolds: references are hidden from the policy and used only to construct problem-specific grading rubrics for judging on-policy reasoning traces. The policy samples from the original problem prompt, while an LLM judge compares the sampled reasoning trace against the reference solution and assigns outcome-level or process-level dense rewards. This lets ExpRL reinforce partial progress, useful intermediate reductions, and productive reasoning behaviors that sparse final-answer rewards often fail to upweight. On challenging math reasoning tasks, ExpRL yields stronger RL priming than SFT, sparse-reward GRPO, and self-distillation, and provides a better initialization for subsequent sparse-reward RL. Additional mixed-domain experiments further suggest that ExpRL can extend beyond the original math-only setting.

ru.title: Автоматическое обучение с подкреплением через человеческие решения как ориентиры для наград
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод ExpRL, который использует человеческие вопросно-ответные данные в качестве шаблонов для создания reward-функций при обучении языковых моделей с подкреплением. Вместо того чтобы напрямую имитировать эталонные решения, модель учится на основе плотных наград, которые оценивают как промежуточные шаги, так и итоговый результат рассуждения. Метод превосходит стандартные подходы предварительного обучения на задачах математического рассуждения и обеспечивает лучшую инициализацию для последующего обучения с разреженными наградами. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что ExpRL может быть применен не только к математическим задачам, но и к задачам из других областей.

en.title: Reinforcing Reasoning: ExpRL Transforms Language Model Training
en.desc: The paper introduces ExpRL, a novel approach that enhances reinforcement learning (RL) for language models by utilizing human-written question-answer pairs as reward scaffolds. Instead of simply imitating reference solutions, ExpRL employs these references to create grading rubrics that evaluate the model's reasoning processes. This method allows the model to receive rewards for partial progress and effective reasoning strategies, which traditional sparse-reward methods often overlook. The results show that ExpRL significantly improves performance on complex math reasoning tasks compared to existing RL techniques, indicating its potential for broader applications beyond math.

zh.title: ExpRL：自动化强化学习的新方法
zh.desc: ExpRL是一种利用人类编写的问题-答案数据作为奖励支架的自动化强化学习方法，旨在提升语言模型在数学推理任务上的表现。与传统方法相比，ExpRL通过隐藏参考答案，仅将其用于构建特定问题的评分标准，从而评估模型的推理过程。该方法允许模型在推理过程中获得密集奖励，强化部分进展和有效的推理行为，而不仅仅依赖于稀疏的最终答案奖励。实验结果表明，ExpRL在复杂的数学推理任务中表现优于其他强化学习方法，并且在其他领域的应用也显示出潜力。

papers.title: EgoPhys: Learning Generalizable Physics Models of Deformable Objects from Egocentric Video
papers.abstract: Abstract EgoPhys enables deformable digital twin generation from egocentric RGB video by using generalizable priors and compact codebooks to predict dense spring stiffness fields without per-spring optimization.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Humans naturally understand object physics through everyday interactions, but faithfully predicting complex deformable dynamics, such as elastic materials and fabrics, remains a major challenge for computer vision and robotics. We present EgoPhys, a framework that constructs deformable physical digital twins from egocentric RGB-only video using generalizable priors. EgoPhys overcomes the limitations of existing methods to enable controllable deformable digital twin generation from egocentric videos by distilling per-object inverse-physics solutions into a compact codebook, enabling prediction of dense spring stiffness fields for unseen objects without per-spring test-time optimization. Trained with generalizable priors from diverse egocentric interactions, EgoPhys outperforms baselines in reconstruction, future prediction, and zero-shot generalization. To support training and evaluation, we curate an egocentric interaction dataset covering diverse deformable objects, scenes, and manipulation styles. We deploy EgoPhys on a real xArm6 robot, demonstrating that a digital twin initialized from a single egocentric human play video can serve as an internal world representation to aid in deformable-object planning, highlighting egocentric RGB observations as a scalable path toward real-to-sim pipelines.

ru.title: Цифровые двойники деформируемых объектов из эгоцентрического видео
ru.desc: EgoPhys — это система для создания деформируемых цифровых двойников объектов из эгоцентрического RGB-видео. Метод использует обобщаемые приоры и компактный кодбук для предсказания плотных полей жёсткости пружин без оптимизации для каждой пружины. Модель обучена на разнообразных эгоцентрических взаимодействиях с деформируемыми объектами и демонстрирует превосходство в реконструкции, предсказании будущего и нулевоподобной генерализации. Система протестирована на роботе xArm6, где цифровой двойник используется как внутреннее представление мира для планирования манипуляций с деформируемыми объектами.

en.title: Revolutionizing Deformable Object Simulation with EgoPhys
en.desc: EgoPhys is a framework that creates digital twins of deformable objects using only RGB video captured from a first-person perspective. It utilizes generalizable priors and a compact codebook to predict the stiffness of materials without needing to optimize each spring individually. This approach allows for better performance in reconstructing and predicting the behavior of unseen objects based on prior interactions. By training on a diverse dataset of egocentric interactions, EgoPhys demonstrates significant improvements in zero-shot generalization and can be applied in robotic planning tasks.

zh.title: EgoPhys：从自我中心视频生成可变形数字双胞胎
zh.desc: EgoPhys 是一个框架，可以从以自我为中心的 RGB 视频生成可变形的数字双胞胎。它利用通用先验和紧凑的代码本，预测密集的弹簧刚度场，而无需对每个弹簧进行优化。EgoPhys 通过提炼每个物体的逆物理解决方案，克服了现有方法的局限性，实现了可控的可变形数字双胞胎生成。经过多样化的自我中心交互训练，EgoPhys 在重建、未来预测和零样本泛化方面超越了基线。

papers.title: Implicit Reasoning for Large Language Model-based Generative Recommendation
papers.abstract: Abstract Large Language Models for generative recommendation face challenges with semantic IDs disrupting natural-language reasoning, prompting a lightweight implicit reasoning approach that outperforms explicit methods while reducing computational costs.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly adopted as backbones for Generative Recommendation (GR), promising access to pretrained world knowledge. Yet reliably invoking this knowledge for GR remains poorly understood. A key obstacle is that LLM-based GR typically represents items with Semantic IDs (SIDs), disrupting LLMs' natural-language reasoning interface because these tokens are unseen by the LLM during pretraining. Existing approaches address this with expensive multi-stage pipelines that ground SIDs and elicit explicit rationales, but offer limited insight into when and why each stage is necessary. In this work, we systematically decompose explicit reasoning training pipelines for LLM-based GR, revealing three key limitations: weakened world-knowledge verbalization, misalignment between SID and natural-language token embedding spaces, and sensitivity to rationale quality, all of which hurt explicit reasoning performance. To circumvent these issues, we propose PauseRec, a lightweight implicit reasoning paradigm tailored for GR. PauseRec is exceptionally practical, avoiding costly reasoning trace acquisition and reasoning alignment training, leading to a multitude of benefits: (1) it outperforms standard explicit CoT methods by up to 6.22%, (2) it reduces training cost by up to 65% GPU hours, and (3) it speeds up inference by up to 71.3%. These results position PauseRec as a lightweight alternative to explicit rationale generation, enabling more effective and efficient LLM-based GR.

ru.title: Лёгкое неявное рассуждение вместо затратного явного — путь к эффективным LLM-рекомендациям
ru.desc: Статья рассматривает использование больших языковых моделей (LLM) для генеративных рекомендаций, выявляя проблему с семантическими идентификаторами предметов, которые нарушают естественный языковой рассуждение моделей. Авторы показывают, что существующие подходы с явными рассуждениями (chain-of-thought) требуют дорогостоящих многоэтапных конвейеров обучения и страдают от нескольких фундаментальных ограничений. В качестве решения предлагается метод PauseRec, использующий неявное рассуждение, которое не требует извлечения и выравнивания трассировок рассуждений. Новый подход превосходит явные методы на 6.22%, сокращает вычислительные затраты на 65% GPU-часов и ускоряет вывод на 71.3%.

en.title: PauseRec: Simplifying Reasoning for Better Recommendations
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenges faced by Large Language Models (LLMs) in generative recommendation systems, particularly due to the use of Semantic IDs (SIDs) that disrupt natural-language reasoning. The authors identify limitations in existing explicit reasoning methods, such as inefficiencies in world-knowledge verbalization and alignment issues between SIDs and natural-language tokens. To overcome these challenges, they introduce PauseRec, a lightweight implicit reasoning approach that simplifies the reasoning process. PauseRec not only improves performance by up to 6.22% but also significantly reduces training costs and speeds up inference, making it a more efficient alternative for LLM-based generative recommendations.

zh.title: 轻量级隐式推理，提升生成推荐效率
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了大型语言模型在生成推荐系统中的应用，特别是如何克服语义ID对自然语言推理的干扰。研究提出了一种轻量级的隐式推理方法PauseRec，能够在减少计算成本的同时，提升生成推荐的性能。与传统的显式推理方法相比，PauseRec在训练成本和推理速度上都有显著优势。通过系统分析现有方法的局限性，本文为生成推荐提供了更有效的解决方案。

papers.title: LaWAM: Latent World Action Models for Efficient Dynamics-Aware Robot Policies
papers.abstract: Abstract LaWAM enables efficient robot control by predicting compact latent visual subgoals instead of expensive video generation, achieving high performance with reduced computational latency.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Vision-Language-Action models (VLAs) leverage large-scale vision-language pretraining for semantic robot control, but often lack explicit foresight into how robot actions change the scene. World-Action Models (WAMs) address this limitation by conditioning policies on predicted futures, yet existing approaches typically rely on computationally expensive video generation with substantial pixel-level redundancy. We present LaWAM, a Latent World Action Model that exposes predictive dynamics to robot policies through compact latent visual subgoals instead of reconstructed future video. At the core of LaWAM is a latent-action-conditioned Latent World Model (LaWM). We obtain LaWM by training a latent action model in the latent space of a pretrained vision foundation model and repurposing its forward decoder to predict future observation features for scene evolution. LaWAM then conditions action generation on these predicted latent visual subgoals to enable dynamics-aware robot control. LaWAM achieves state-of-the-art or competitive success rates (SRs) across LIBERO (98.6% SR), RoboTwin (91.22% SR), and real-world manipulation tasks while retaining low-latency inference. LaWAM runs in 187 ms per action-chunk prediction and achieves up to 24x lower wall-clock latency than pixel-space WAMs.

ru.title: Быстрое управление роботом через предсказание скрытых подцелей
ru.desc: LaWAM представляет собой модель для управления роботами, которая предсказывает компактные промежуточные цели в скрытом пространстве вместо дорогостоящей генерации видео. Авторы обучают модель динамики в латентном пространстве предварительно обученной vision foundation model, используя её декодер для предсказания будущих признаков сцены. Политика робота генерирует действия, основываясь на предсказанных латентных визуальных подцелях, что обеспечивает управление с явным учётом динамики окружения. Подход достигает передовых результатов на бенчмарках LIBERO и RoboTwin при 24-кратном снижении вычислительной задержки по сравнению с пиксельными world-action models.

en.title: Efficient Robot Control with Latent Visual Subgoals
en.desc: LaWAM is a novel approach for robot control that enhances efficiency by predicting compact latent visual subgoals instead of generating full video sequences. This method leverages large-scale vision-language pretraining to improve semantic understanding in robot actions while avoiding the computational costs associated with traditional video generation. By utilizing a Latent World Model (LaWM), LaWAM conditions robot actions on predicted future observations, allowing for more informed decision-making. The results show that LaWAM achieves high success rates in various tasks while significantly reducing latency compared to existing methods.

zh.title: 高效机器人控制的新方法：LaWAM
zh.desc: LaWAM是一种高效的机器人控制方法，通过预测紧凑的潜在视觉子目标来替代昂贵的视频生成，从而实现高性能和低计算延迟。它利用大规模的视觉-语言预训练模型，解决了现有方法在机器人动作如何改变场景方面缺乏明确前瞻性的问题。LaWAM的核心是潜在动作条件的潜在世界模型（LaWM），通过在预训练的视觉基础模型的潜在空间中训练潜在动作模型来获得。最终，LaWAM通过这些预测的潜在视觉子目标来条件化动作生成，从而实现动态感知的机器人控制。

papers.title: Who Flips? Self- and Cross-Model Counterarguments Reveal Answer Instability in LLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract Answer stability in large language models is evaluated through controlled challenges that measure response consistency when correct answers face plausible counterarguments, revealing significant variation in model reliability beyond traditional accuracy metrics.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Standard accuracy benchmarks are designed to test how closely large language models (LLMs) approach correct answers, but are not suitable for testing whether LLMs stick with a correct answer when that answer is challenged by a plausible counter-argument. We introduce a controlled protocol for evaluating answer stability: after a model answers a multiple-choice question correctly, we challenge the model's answer with a coherent argument for an incorrect option and measure whether the model flips. The setup a) isolates argumentative content from overt social pressure and b) varies argument length, self-attribution, and cross-model source. Across seven frontier models and 57 MMLU subjects, flip rates range from 17.5% to 97.3%, revealing large differences in stability that are not captured by accuracy metrics alone. We find that self-attribution consistently increases flip rates (mean +7.1pp, up to +18.7pp). Also, pooling wrong-answer arguments across models and selecting the most effective one per question yields stronger adversarial challenges than relying on any single source model. We further construct MaxFlip, a curated challenge set that amplifies flips by up to +23.6pp over standard self-generated challenges. We release the protocol, challenge records, and MaxFlip to support stability evaluation alongside standard accuracy benchmarks. Materials are available at https://github.com/nafisenik/WhoFlips and https://hf.co/datasets/nafisehNik/WhoFlips.

ru.title: Устойчивость к возражениям: когда модели предают свои верные ответы
ru.desc: В статье предлагается новый метод оценки стабильности больших языковых моделей, который измеряет, остаются ли модели верны своему ответу при наличии убедительных контраргументов. Исследователи разработали контролируемый протокол, в котором после правильного ответа на вопрос модель подвергается аргументированному возражению и измеряется, изменит ли она свой ответ. Результаты показывают огромную вариативность в стабильности моделей (от 17,5% до 97,3% смены ответов), которая не отражается традиционными метриками точности. Авторы создали набор данных MaxFlip, специально отобранный для усиления эффекта смены ответов, что позволит лучше оценивать надёжность моделей помимо стандартных бенчмарков.

en.title: Evaluating Stability: Beyond Accuracy in Language Models
en.desc: This paper evaluates the stability of large language models (LLMs) by testing how consistently they maintain correct answers when faced with plausible counterarguments. Traditional accuracy metrics do not capture this aspect of model performance, so the authors introduce a new protocol that challenges models after they provide correct answers. They find significant variability in how often models change their answers, with flip rates ranging from 17.5% to 97.3%. The study also shows that certain factors, like self-attribution, can influence these flip rates, and they provide a curated challenge set called MaxFlip to enhance the evaluation process.

zh.title: 评估大型语言模型答案稳定性的新方法
zh.desc: 本文探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）在面对合理反驳时的答案稳定性。研究表明，传统的准确性指标无法全面反映模型在挑战下的可靠性。我们提出了一种控制协议，通过对模型的正确答案进行反驳，测量模型是否会改变答案。结果显示，不同模型的答案翻转率差异显著，且自我归因会显著提高翻转率。

papers.title: Selective Control under Noisy Perception: Governance Failures Hidden by Aggregate Metrics in Modular Networks
papers.abstract: Abstract Content moderation systems can cause disproportionate harm to bridge users connecting separate communities, even when overall accuracy metrics appear satisfactory, with governance loss increasing significantly under false-positive-heavy conditions.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct A content-moderation system can score well on every standard accuracy metric and still cause real harm, if its mistakes fall on the few users who connect otherwise separate communities. We show this in an agent-based model where N=240 learning agents on a community-structured network each post harmless, productive, or dangerous content, and a regulator removes or penalizes whatever a noisy classifier flags. Overall usefulness barely moves as the noise changes (one-way ANOVA, p=0.96): by aggregate measures, nothing looks wrong. The damage instead concentrates on these bridge users, whose useful posts are wrongly suppressed and whose dangerous posts are wrongly spared. A governance loss (L_gov) that prices these two mistakes separately from the cost of enforcement more than doubles under false-positive-heavy noise. Aggregate accuracy hides who is harmed, and the cheap quantity to audit is how many connections a user has (degree), a near-perfect proxy for the betweenness that defines a bridge (r=0.96).

ru.title: Метрики лгут: скрытые жертвы систем модерации контента на сетевых структурах
ru.desc: В статье исследуется проблема систем модерации контента, которые показывают хорошие метрики точности в целом, но наносят непропорциональный вред пользователям-мостам, соединяющим отдельные сообщества. Авторы используют агентную модель с 240 обучающимися агентами на сетевой структуре, где классификатор удаляет или штрафует контент, вызывая концентрированный ущерб именно для мостов. Показано, что общие метрики точности скрывают, кому именно наносится вред: полезные посты мостов подавляются, а опасные - пропускаются. Функция потерь управления, учитывающая эти две ошибки отдельно, более чем удваивается при высокой доле ложноположительных результатов, хотя степень узла пользователя является надежным прокси для выявления мостов.

en.title: Content Moderation: Hidden Harm to Community Connectors
en.desc: This paper discusses the unintended consequences of content moderation systems that may appear accurate overall but can disproportionately harm users who connect different communities. It highlights that even with high accuracy metrics, the system can fail to protect bridge users, leading to governance loss when false positives are prevalent. The authors use an agent-based model to demonstrate that while aggregate measures of usefulness remain stable, the real damage is concentrated on these critical users. They propose that auditing user connections can serve as an effective proxy for identifying bridge users who are most affected by moderation errors.

zh.title: 内容审核的隐患：桥接用户的伤害
zh.desc: 内容审核系统可能对连接不同社区的用户造成不成比例的伤害，即使整体准确性指标看起来令人满意。在错误积极性较高的情况下，治理损失显著增加。我们通过一个基于代理的模型展示了这一点，其中240个学习代理在社区结构网络中发布内容。虽然整体有用性几乎没有变化，但错误的内容审核会错误地抑制桥接用户的有用帖子，导致这些用户受到严重影响。

papers.title: Attacks on Machine-Text Detectors Retain Stylistic Fingerprints
papers.abstract: Abstract Machine-text detection remains challenging despite evasion techniques, but stylistic features can provide robust defense when analyzed across multiple documents rather than individual instances.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Despite considerable progress in the development of machine-text detectors, the ease with which machine-text can be manipulated to evade detection has led to suggestions that the problem is inherently intractable. In this work, we investigate the limits of such evasion strategies. We demonstrate that while current attacks, ranging from prompt engineering to detector-guided optimization can effectively degrade performance of standard detectors, they fail to erase the underlying stylistic "fingerprints" of machine text. We show that few-shot detectors that utilize the stylistic feature space are robust to these evasion attempts, reliably detecting samples even from models explicitly tuned to prevent detection. This raises the question: does style represent a universal defense against machine-detection attacks? We demonstrate that the answer is "no'' by introducing a novel paraphrasing approach that simultaneously optimizes for undetectability and adherence to specific human styles. We show that unlike prior methods, this attack effectively evades all considered detectors, including those that utilize writing style. However, we find that this evasion is not absolute: as the number of documents available for analysis grows, the human and machine distributions become distinguishable again. Overall, our findings suggest that reliable machine-text detection requires moving beyond single-document analysis to multi-document analysis.

ru.title: Защита от поддельных текстов через анализ стиля на множестве документов
ru.desc: В работе исследуется проблема обнаружения текстов, сгенерированных языковыми моделями, и методы их скрытия. Авторы показывают, что стилистические особенности машинного текста остаются узнаваемыми даже при попытках манипуляции через prompt engineering и оптимизацию. Они демонстрируют, что детекторы, анализирующие стилистический паттерн, устойчивы к атакам, но предлагают новый метод парафразирования, который одновременно оптимизирует незаметность и соответствие человеческому стилю. Ключевой вывод: надежное обнаружение машинного текста требует анализа множества документов одновременно, а не отдельных экземпляров.

en.title: Style Matters: Multi-Document Analysis for Robust Machine-Text Detection
en.desc: This paper explores the challenges of detecting machine-generated text, particularly in light of evasion techniques that can manipulate text to avoid detection. It highlights that while current evasion strategies can reduce the effectiveness of standard detectors, they do not eliminate the unique stylistic features of machine-generated text. The authors propose that few-shot detectors, which analyze stylistic features across multiple documents, can still identify machine text despite these evasion attempts. However, they also introduce a new paraphrasing method that can evade detection by optimizing for both undetectability and specific human writing styles, suggesting that effective detection requires analyzing multiple documents rather than relying on single instances.

zh.title: 风格特征：机器文本检测的关键防线
zh.desc: 本文探讨了机器文本检测的挑战，尤其是如何应对规避技术。尽管现有的规避策略可以降低检测器的性能，但它们无法完全消除机器文本的风格特征。我们提出了一种新颖的改写方法，能够同时优化不可检测性和特定人类风格的遵循。研究表明，随着分析文档数量的增加，人类和机器文本的分布再次可区分，因此有效的机器文本检测需要多文档分析。

papers.title: Human Universal Grasping
papers.abstract: Abstract A flow-matching model generates diverse human grasps from RGB-D images, enabling zero-shot robotic grasping with improved performance over existing methods.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Humans can grasp objects effortlessly, whereas multi-fingered robots are far from this level of generality. We argue that the most natural source of robot grasping data is from humans, who pick up thousands of objects every day. We present HUG, a flow-matching model that generates diverse human grasps for any user-specified object in a single RGB-D image captured from a stereo camera. Using smart glasses, we first collect 1M-HUGs, an egocentric dataset of human grasps spanning 1M frames (27.8 hrs) and 6,707 object instances across 41 buildings. Next, to model the distribution of natural human grasps, our novel flow-matching model fuses RGB and depth observations to output a grasp parameterized by wrist translation, wrist rotation, and MANO hand pose. Predicted grasps can be retargeted to various robot hands, enabling zero-shot grasping in everyday scenes. To standardize evaluation, we build a new simulated benchmark, HUG-Bench, of 90 unseen objects from five geometric categories and various sizes, with metric-scale 3D meshes. We evaluate HUG in the real world on the 30-object test set of HUG-Bench across multiple stereo cameras, robot embodiments, and household environments. HUG outperforms the state-of-the-art grasping baselines by +23% and +34% on our challenging object set. Code, data, benchmark, checkpoints, and an interactive demo are released on our website: https://grasping.io/

ru.title: Обучение роботов захватыванию через генеративное моделирование человеческих движений
ru.desc: В статье представлена модель HUG на основе flow-matching, которая генерирует разнообразные схемы захватывания объектов человеком по RGB-D изображениям со стереокамер. Авторы собрали датасет 1M-HUGs из 1 миллиона эгоцентричных кадров видео со смарт-очков, содержащий естественные захватывания людьми различных предметов. Модель объединяет RGB и глубинные данные для предсказания параметров захвата (положение и ориентация запястья, поза руки MANO) и может быть адаптирована к различным роботизированным манипуляторам. Система достигает улучшения на 23-34% по сравнению с существующими методами на новом бенчмарке HUG-Bench с 90 невидимыми объектами в реальных бытовых сценариях.

en.title: HUG: Bridging Human Grasping and Robotic Precision
en.desc: The paper introduces HUG, a flow-matching model that generates diverse human grasps from RGB-D images, facilitating zero-shot robotic grasping. It leverages a large dataset of 1M human grasps collected through smart glasses to model the distribution of natural human grasping behavior. The model outputs grasp parameters that can be adapted to different robotic hands, enhancing the versatility of robotic grasping in everyday scenarios. HUG demonstrates significant performance improvements over existing methods, achieving higher accuracy in grasping tasks across various objects and environments.

zh.title: 人类抓取数据驱动的机器人抓取新突破
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种流匹配模型HUG，可以从RGB-D图像中生成多样化的人类抓取动作，从而实现零-shot的机器人抓取。该模型通过融合RGB和深度信息，输出由手腕平移、手腕旋转和手部姿态参数化的抓取动作。我们收集了一个包含100万帧人类抓取数据的自我中心数据集，并在多个真实场景中对HUG进行了评估。实验结果表明，HUG在抓取性能上比现有方法提高了23%到34%。

papers.title: The Ghosts of Polymarket: When Off-Chain Matches Meet On-Chain Reverts
papers.abstract: Abstract  Polymarket has emerged as a prominent prediction market platform and one of the fastest-growing applications in DeFi. To achieve low-latency trading, it adopts a hybrid architecture that matches orders off-chain but settles them on-chain for final execution. This design creates a consistency gap we call Ghost Fills: an order that is successfully matched off-chain may later fail during on-chain settlement. To understand the security implications of this gap, we investigate such failed settlements by building GHOSTHUNTER, which reconstructs them from on-chain traces and attributes to concrete attack patterns. Across 1,952,440 reverted match-order transactions, we find that attackers exploit the time gap between matching and settlement to invalidate already matched orders before they are finalized on-chain. We then identify four attack vectors from these incidents: nonce bump, balance drain, allowance revoke, and proxy trap, realized via 35 evolving variants. These vectors allow attackers to selectively revert 980,133 filled orders, enabling risk-free prediction, arbitrage-bot hunting, and liquidity reward manipulation, realizing at least \1.49M in profit, which places 1.78 B USD at risk and 2.17 M POL (about \212 K) paid by operator. During peak hours, more than 24.3% of all filled orders reverted, causing de facto DoS attacks. We also find that code derived from the flawed contract still appears in 167 independent contracts across 10 chains holding at least 23 M in user funds, extending the impact beyond Polymarket. We have disclosed our evidence to affected parties, and the issue has been partially mitigated.

ru.title: Призраки в машине: как временные разрывы в блокчейн-системах становятся золотой жилой для атакующих
ru.desc: Авторы исследуют уязвимости в архитектуре платформы Polymarket, которая использует гибридный подход с согласованием ордеров вне цепи и финальным расчётом в блокчейне. Они разработали инструмент GHOSTHUNTER для анализа неудачных расчётов и выявили четыре основных вектора атак: изменение nonce, дренирование баланса, отзыв разрешения и ловушка прокси. Злоумышленники эксплуатируют временной промежуток между согласованием и поселением, чтобы аннулировать уже согласованные ордера, получая прибыль через беспроигрышные стратегии и манипуляцию наградами ликвидности. Обнаруженные уязвимости способны привести к потере более 1,49 млн долларов и выявляют системный риск для экосистемы DeFi в целом.

en.title: Unveiling Ghost Fills: Securing Prediction Markets Against Exploits
en.desc: This paper investigates the security vulnerabilities in Polymarket, a prediction market platform that uses a hybrid architecture for trading. The authors introduce the concept of 'Ghost Fills', where orders matched off-chain may fail during on-chain settlement, leading to potential exploits. They develop a tool called GHOSTHUNTER to analyze these failed settlements and identify attack patterns that exploit the time gap between order matching and finalization. The study reveals significant risks, including the ability for attackers to revert filled orders, resulting in substantial financial losses and risks to user funds across multiple blockchain networks.

zh.title: 揭示预测市场中的安全隐患
zh.desc: Polymarket 是一个快速发展的预测市场平台，采用混合架构以实现低延迟交易。该架构在链下匹配订单，但在链上进行最终结算，这导致了我们称之为“幽灵填充”的一致性差距。我们开发了 GHOSTHUNTER 工具，分析了失败的链上结算，发现攻击者利用匹配和结算之间的时间差来撤销已匹配的订单。通过识别四种攻击向量，我们揭示了这些攻击如何导致大量订单被撤销，给用户资金带来了风险。

papers.title: TuneJury: An Open Metric for Improving Music Generation Preference Alignment
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel open-source pairwise reward model for text-to-music generation that provides calibrated preference scoring and generalizes across multiple downstream applications through a frozen reward mechanism.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We introduce TuneJury, an open, instance-level pairwise reward model for text-to-music that predicts a music preference score from a text prompt and an audio clip. The released checkpoint is trained on publicly available human-preference labels covering arena-style (A vs. B) votes, metric-alignment preference pairs, crowdsourced pairwise comparisons, and expert aesthetic ratings. The predicted score margin between two clips is well calibrated on our held-out test split, supporting data filtering via a simple score threshold. TuneJury generalizes to both held-out test pairs and out-of-distribution benchmarks, remaining competitive with prior baselines on the latter. For generators released after training, we introduce anchor calibration, a post-hoc, per-system Bradley-Terry calibration that recovers agreement at substantially better data efficiency than from-scratch retraining. The same frozen reward drives consistent reward-axis gains across three downstream applications: inference-time best-of-N selection, DITTO-style latent optimization, and expert-iteration post-training. TuneJury is available at https://github.com/yonghyunk1m/TuneJury.

ru.title: Универсальная замороженная модель вознаграждения для оценки качества синтеза музики
ru.desc: В статье представляется TuneJury — открытая модель вознаграждения (reward model) для оценки качества генерируемой музыки на основе текстового описания. Модель обучена на различных типах предпочтений человека, включая сравнительные оценки, метрики качества и экспертные оценки, что позволяет ей давать калиброванные предсказания. Авторы показывают, что замороженная (frozen) модель вознаграждения хорошо обобщается на новые данные и может быть адаптирована к новым генераторам музыки через якорную калибровку без переобучения. Одна и та же модель вознаграждения успешно применяется для трёх различных задач: отбора лучших вариантов, оптимизации скрытого пространства и итеративного обучения экспертов.

en.title: TuneJury: Calibrated Music Generation from Text Prompts
en.desc: This paper presents TuneJury, an innovative open-source pairwise reward model designed for generating music from text prompts. It utilizes a calibrated preference scoring system that evaluates audio clips based on human preferences, allowing it to generalize effectively across various applications. The model is trained on diverse datasets, including expert ratings and crowdsourced comparisons, ensuring robust performance on unseen data. Additionally, it introduces a calibration method that enhances efficiency in aligning music generation systems with user preferences without the need for extensive retraining.

zh.title: TuneJury：文本到音乐生成的新型奖励模型
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种新颖的开源成对奖励模型TuneJury，用于文本到音乐的生成。该模型能够根据文本提示和音频片段预测音乐偏好分数，并且在多个下游应用中具有良好的泛化能力。TuneJury通过冻结奖励机制实现了偏好评分的校准，并在测试集上表现出色。该模型还引入了锚定校准技术，提升了数据效率，适用于多种生成任务。
